summaryText: Pastor Travis Hearn delivered a passionate message titled 'Don't Bow to Baal,' framed as a 'bonus track' between verses 2 and 3 of Psalm 23. He taught that spiritual warfare intensifies right after a victory or mountaintop experience, using the examples of Elijah's fear after defeating the prophets of Baal and Jesus's temptation after his baptism. The sermon called for a definitive, uncompromising stand for God, confronting the 'Baals' of modern culture—such as the idols of comfort, success, lust, and popularity—which represent spiritual adultery. Pastor Travis emphasized that there is no middle ground; believers must choose to serve Yahweh alone. He provided three directives: don't conform to culture but confront it, understand the power of holy fire that falls only on the altar of personal sacrifice, and stand strong in faith even if standing alone. The message concluded with an altar call, inviting listeners to surrender their lives fully to Christ, receive His unconditional love, and live as a consecrated, living sacrifice.
mainPoints: Spiritual warfare often attacks after a victory, and believers must refuse to compromise or 'bow to Baal'—the false gods of modern culture.
God's holy fire and power are released through personal sacrifice and surrender at the altar, not through empty religious performance.
Courageous faith requires standing strong for God's truth, even when it means standing alone against cultural pressure.

versesMentioned.text: The Lord is my shepherd, I lack nothing. He makes me lie down in green pastures, he leads me beside quiet waters, he refreshes my soul. He guides me along the right paths for his name’s sake. Even though I walk through the darkest valley, I will fear no evil, for you are with me; your rod and your staff, they comfort me. You prepare a table before me in the presence of my enemies. You anoint my head with oil; my cup overflows. Surely your goodness and love will follow me all the days of my life, and I will dwell in the house of the Lord forever.
Again, the devil took him to a very high mountain and showed him all the kingdoms of the world and their splendor. 'All this I will give you,' he said, 'if you will bow down and worship me.'
Elijah went before the people and said, 'How long will you waver between two opinions? If the Lord is God, follow him; but if Baal is God, follow him.' But the people said nothing.
I know your deeds, that you are neither cold nor hot. I wish you were either one or the other! So, because you are lukewarm—neither hot nor cold—I am about to spit you out of my mouth.
having a form of godliness but denying its power. Have nothing to do with such people.

Put on the full armor of God, so that you can take your stand against the devil’s schemes. For our struggle is not against flesh and blood, but against the rulers, against the authorities, against the powers of this dark world and against the spiritual forces of evil in the heavenly realms. Therefore put on the full armor of God, so that when the day of evil comes, you may be able to stand your ground, and after you have done everything, to stand.

Therefore, I urge you, brothers and sisters, in view of God’s mercy, to offer your bodies as a living sacrifice, holy and pleasing to God—this is your true and proper worship. Do not conform to the pattern of this world, but be transformed by the renewing of your mind. Then you will be able to test and approve what God’s will is—his good, pleasing and perfect will.

Monday.scripture: 1 Kings 18:21 (NIV) - 'How long will you waver between two opinions? If the Lord is God, follow him; but if Baal is God, follow him.'
Monday.focus: Identify one area of compromise in your life.
Monday.action: Write down one 'Baal' (e.g., comfort, others' opinions, a secret sin) you have been bowing to and make a conscious decision to stop wavering.
Monday.prayer: Lord, give me clarity and courage to choose You fully today. Expose any area where I am compromising and help me to stand for You alone.

Tuesday.scripture: Matthew 4:10 (NIV) - 'Jesus said to him, 'Away from me, Satan! For it is written: ‘Worship the Lord your God, and serve him only.’''
Tuesday.action: When faced with a temptation today, speak a scripture out loud, just as Jesus did.
Tuesday.prayer: Jesus, when the tempter comes, remind me of Your Word. Strengthen me to worship and serve only You.

Wednesday.scripture: Romans 12:1 (NIV) - 'Therefore, I urge you, brothers and sisters, in view of God’s mercy, to offer your bodies as a living sacrifice, holy and pleasing to God—this is your true and proper worship.'
Wednesday.action: Intentionally lay down one personal desire or plan today as an act of surrender to God's will.
Wednesday.prayer: God, I offer myself to You as a living sacrifice. My life is Your altar. Consume me with Your holy fire.

Thursday.scripture: Ephesians 6:13 (NIV) - 'Therefore put on the full armor of God, so that when the day of evil comes, you may be able to stand your ground, and after you have done everything, to stand.'
Thursday.focus: Stand your ground in spiritual warfare.
Thursday.action: Put on the 'armor of God' by praying through each piece (truth, righteousness, gospel, faith, salvation, Word) for protection today.
Thursday.prayer: Heavenly Father, I put on Your full armor. Help me to stand firm against every attack and scheme of the enemy.

Friday.scripture: Daniel 3:17-18 (NIV) - 'If we are thrown into the blazing furnace, the God we serve is able to deliver us from it... But even if he does not... we will not serve your gods.'
Friday.action: Reflect on a current challenge. Declare your trust in God's ability to deliver you, and also your commitment to Him even if He doesn't act as you hope.
Friday.prayer: God, my faith is in You alone. Even if You don't deliver me the way I want, I will not bow. I trust You in the fire.

Saturday.scripture: Hebrews 12:29 (NIV) - 'for our ‘God is a consuming fire.’'
Saturday.action: Spend time in quiet prayer, asking God to consume any impurity, fear, or idol in your heart with His holy fire.
Saturday.prayer: Consuming Fire, burn away everything in me that is not of You. Purify my heart and let Your fire fall on my altar today.
